text:
Team GBis set to have another young star aiming for gold in the Women's Park Skateboarding event at theOlympic Games, with Lola Tambling joining Sky Brown as a medal contender.

Brown, alreadya household nameamong skateboarding enthusiasts,clinched bronzeat the Tokyo Games in 2021 at the tender age of 13. This remarkable feat made her Britain's youngest-ever medallist.

Now aged 16, Brown will be accompanied by rising star Tambling. The young Cornwall native began her meteoric rise when she was crowned British national champion in 2022. Since the she has cemented her status as a talent to watch this summer by securing sixth place at the World Championships in Sharjah last year and holding the 13th spot in global rankings, as reported inThe Mirror.

Alongside Brown, and veteran skater Andy MacDonald, aged 51, the trio are set to make their mark on the podium when they take to their boards this week. The three hopefuls navigated a two-part Olympic Qualifying Series (OQS) to secure their spots in Paris.

Impressively, Tambling managed to amass enough points to qualify as one of the top 22 skaters even without competing in the final. From a tender age of seven, Tambling took to the skateboard like a natural after her father Martyn, himself a professional BMX rider, constructed a skate park for her.By the age of 11, she was already clinching titles in competitions, and although she had an affinity for her father's sport, skateboarding ultimately captured her heart.

"I've always grown up around the BMX and skateboarding scene," Tambling shared withTeam GB's website. "Maybe I would have done BMXing but I was like, nah, skating's the one."



Witnessing her team-mate Brown secure an Olympic bronze in 2021 was a pivotal moment for Tambling. Bolstered by Brown's success and her own burgeoning track record, she began to trust in her own convictions that has now catapulted her to the pinnacle of sport, the Olympic Games.

Reflecting on her performance at the World Championships in Sharjah where she placed sixth, Tambling expressed her newfound confidence: "Last year in Sharjah, when I came sixth at the World Champs, I thought I'm actually up there, I'm good enough, I'm with all the top (skateboarders)," she said. "This is the start and if I keep going, I could definitely make it. Because I've made it into the top 20, I'm in a really good spot."

The meteoric ascent of the talented young star has seen her become a sensation not only on the half-pipe but also on the fashion runway, collaborating with iconic brands such as Ben Sherman and Adidas. Despite her success outside of the sport, she's made it abundantly clear that skateboarding, particularly the Olympics, remains her ultimate ambition, as perThe Daily Star.

With her sights firmly set on Paris, she expressed her dedication: "Paris is my main focus. Everything I am doing now is to build up to the Olympics. Everything I'm doing is for me to get to the Olympics. That's the main goal so I'm always thinking about it."

Skateboarding's inclusion in the Olympic Games is a recent development, having only debuted in Tokyo, and Tambling shared her excitement about the sport's newfound recognition: "It was really sick to see it in the Olympics. We were watching Sky and cheering her on, she did really well. It's so cool, it's really crazy."

As Paris 2024 approaches, Tambling will be among an impressive lineup of youthful skateboarders vying for glory. The women's street finals saw Japan's 14-year-old Coco Yoshizawa take gold, while compatriot, 15-year-old Liz Akama, and Brazil's 16-year-old Rayssa Leal secured silver and bronze, respectively.

Tambling, alongside fellow competitor Brown, will compete in the women's park skateboarding preliminaries at 11.30am on Tuesday, August 6. Success in the preliminaries could see them battle for medals in the final later that day at 4.30pm.

title: Who is Team GB's 16-year-old skateboard star Lola Tambling? | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Chris Burns
description: Lola Tambling will be going for gold at the Olympic Games and is hoping to follow in the footsteps of her Team GB team-mate Sky Brown.
